sections.biography-source-undefined:
Huang Hua was from Jiuquan. It is unknown if he is the same person as the Huang Hua who served as Inspector of Yan Province in 251 with the sole evidence being a lone Pei Songzhi annotation claiming that they are.

Huang Hua was most likely a nominal vassal of Cao Cao. However, in 219, after the loss of Hanzhong to Liu Bei, Huang Hua, along with Yan Jun (顏俊), He Luan, and Qu Yan rebelled against Cao Cao and declared themselves Generals. Soon, they started attacking each other. As Huang Hua would rebel later, it seemed that as soon Yan Jun and He Luan were killed, Huang Hua surrendered. After Cao Cao died in 220, though, he, Zhang Jin, and Qu Yan rebelled. Huang Hua denied the Grand Administrator of Jiuquan; Xin Ji (辛機) and instead declared himself the Administrator. Earlier, the acting Grand Administrator; Zhang Gong had asked Cao Cao for a new Grand Administrator and so sent his son, Zhang Jiu. However, when Zhang Jiu arrived in Jiuquan, he was quickly held hostage by Huang Hua. In response, Zhang Gong sent his cousin, Zhang Hua (not Maoxian) to take Shatou and Qianqi Counties from Huang Hua and trapped Zhang Jin. Huang Hua intended to rescue Zhang Jin, however, Su Ze and Zhang Ji (Derong) were able to kill Zhang Jin and Qu Yan, so Huang Hua surrendered out of fear.

full_text:
Huang Hua 黃華
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Huang Hua (黃華)was born in Jiuquan. Warlord of Jiuquan. Due to the death of another warlord, he surrendered to Wei. Huang Hua was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Huang Hua was from Jiuquan. It is unknown if he is the same person as the Huang Hua who served as Inspector of Yan Province in 251 with the sole evidence being a lone Pei Songzhi annotation claiming that they are.
Huang Hua was most likely a nominal vassal of Cao Cao. However, in 219, after the loss of Hanzhong to Liu Bei, Huang Hua, along with Yan Jun (顏俊), He Luan, and Qu Yan rebelled against Cao Cao and declared themselves Generals. Soon, they started attacking each other. As Huang Hua would rebel later, it seemed that as soon Yan Jun and He Luan were killed, Huang Hua surrendered. After Cao Cao died in 220, though, he, Zhang Jin, and Qu Yan rebelled. Huang Hua denied the Grand Administrator of Jiuquan; Xin Ji (辛機) and instead declared himself the Administrator. Earlier, the acting Grand Administrator; Zhang Gong had asked Cao Cao for a new Grand Administrator and so sent his son, Zhang Jiu. However, when Zhang Jiu arrived in Jiuquan, he was quickly held hostage by Huang Hua. In response, Zhang Gong sent his cousin, Zhang Hua (not Maoxian) to take Shatou and Qianqi Counties from Huang Hua and trapped Zhang Jin. Huang Hua intended to rescue Zhang Jin, however, Su Ze and Zhang Ji (Derong) were able to kill Zhang Jin and Qu Yan, so Huang Hua surrendered out of fear.
